nodes.label: Local-Only Execution
nodes.rationale: No wrapper, no proxy, no API keys. CodeBurn reads session data directly from disk to ensure user privacy and zero external dependencies.

nodes.label: No AI Co-Author Policy
nodes.rationale: Contributors may use AI tools to help write code but must remove co-author attribution before PR is eligible to merge, per project contributor guidelines.

nodes.label: Resident Process for Caching
nodes.rationale: A resident in-process server is required so the existing 180s session cache amortizes across tool calls; exec-per-call costs ~17.6s even warm due to process teardown.

nodes.label: Split Cheap vs Optimize Path
nodes.rationale: optimize pass (scanAndDetect) accounts for ~70% of total cost (~13s); splitting cheap usage from expensive savings avoids paying that tax on every tool call.

nodes.label: Default Name Redaction
nodes.rationale: MCP is an egress surface to possibly-remote agents; raw repo names + dated spend would egress. Hashing keeps breakdowns coherent without leaking identity; local users opt in per call.

nodes.label: Status/Menubar Parity
nodes.rationale: buildUsage/buildSavings signature carries provider/project/range filters to maintain parity with the existing status --format menubar-json output.

nodes.label: MCP Performance Design
nodes.rationale: Two paths (cheap usage vs expensive optimize), resident process with 180s cache, in-flight coalescing, pre-warm on boot, and token discipline via limit caps keep latency manageable.
